D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Mini-LED Local Dimming

thousands of tiny backlight zones chase deep blacks — but a bright object on black still leaks a soft halo the size of one zone

A Mini-LED LCD splits its backlight into hundreds or thousands of independently dimmed zones. A zone goes dark where the picture is black, lifting contrast far above an edge-lit panel. But the LCD layer can't fully block a lit zone, so a bright star on a black field is ringed by "blooming" — a glow as wide as one backlight zone.

Raise the zone count and the halo around the bright mark tightens toward it. Switch to a self-emissive OLED, where every pixel is its own light, and the bloom vanishes entirely — black is simply off.
